subj: A gentler kind of New Year's resolution
pre: This year, you don't have to do it all alone.
head: What if this year, you asked for a little help?
hero: senior and caregiver laughing over morning coffee

blocks.text: Every January brings a flurry of resolutions, eat better, move more, worry less. But if you've been quietly carrying the weight of caring for an aging parent or spouse, here's one worth adding to the list: let someone help.

At Caring Companions, “help” doesn't mean handing over control. It means a trusted caregiver who folds the laundry, drives to appointments, and shares a cup of coffee, so you can go back to being a daughter, a son, or a spouse instead of the on-call everything.
No contracts. No rigid packages. Just the hours and support that fit your family, adjusted any time life changes.
We've walked alongside more than 400 Southwest Missouri families since 2017. We'd be honored to walk alongside yours.

subj: The quiet danger of a long Missouri winter
pre: Loneliness can be as hard on health as a chronic illness.
head: When the days get short, watch for the quiet signs
hero: older adult by a frosted winter window, warm interior

blocks.text: Winter in Southwest Missouri can keep anyone indoors, but for an older adult living alone, weeks of gray skies and icy sidewalks can slide quietly into isolation. And loneliness isn't just sad; research links it to higher risks of depression, cognitive decline, and heart trouble.
A few things to gently look for in the parent or grandparent you love:

items: Less interest in hobbies, phone calls, or the church they used to enjoy
Skipped meals, or a fridge that stays full
Wearing the same clothes, or letting tidy routines slip
A flatter, quieter mood than usual

blocks.text: Sometimes the fix is simply a familiar face a few times a week, someone to share a meal, a walk, or a memory. That's the heart of what our caregivers do.
Can't visit as often as you'd like? HomeTogether™ brings a friendly check-in right through the TV, starting at $80 a month.

subj: Love is showing up, even when you can't
pre: A little companionship goes a long way this February.
head: The most loving thing isn't always something you can do yourself
hero: caregiver and senior playing cards together, laughing

blocks.text: This Valentine's season, love might look less like flowers and more like making sure Mom isn't spending another afternoon alone. If you live across town, or across the country, that can be a hard truth to sit with.

Companionship care exists for exactly this: a caregiver who genuinely enjoys your loved one's stories, plays the card games they love, and notices when something's off, so the people you love feel seen, even on the days you can't be there.
I stopped worrying every evening. Knowing someone was with Dad, really with him, gave our whole family peace.
Because at Caring Companions, our caregivers don't just provide a service. They extend the warmth of family.

subj: A few small things that protect an aging heart
pre: February is American Heart Month, here's what helps at home.
head: Heart-healthy living gets harder alone, and easier with a hand
hero: a healthy home-cooked meal being shared at the table

blocks.text: Eating well, staying active, taking medications on time, the basics of heart health sound simple, until age, mobility, or memory get in the way. American Heart Month is a good moment to check in on the daily habits that keep an older heart strong.

items: Home-cooked, lower-sodium meals instead of whatever's easiest
A daily walk, even just around the living room
Medications taken correctly, and on schedule
Fewer stressful errands and more restful days

blocks.text: These are exactly the everyday things our caregivers help with, quietly, consistently, in the background of a normal day.

subj: Noticed something during your last visit?
pre: The signs are easy to miss, until you know what to look for.
head: Trust that little voice that said, “something’s different”
hero: adult child visiting an aging parent at home

blocks.text: Often it's a visit that does it, you walk into your parent's home and something feels off. Mail piling up. A dent in the car nobody mentioned. Less food in the fridge than there should be. It's easy to talk yourself out of it. Please don't.
Common signs it may be time for a little support:

items: Unopened bills, or unusual mistakes with money
New bruises, or unsteadiness on their feet
Forgotten medications or doubled-up doses
Weight loss, or expired food in the pantry
Withdrawing from friends and favorite activities

blocks.text: Noticing early is a gift. It means you can put gentle support in place now, and help your loved one stay safe and independent at home far longer.

subj: Care and connection, right through the TV
pre: Meet HomeTogether™, starting at $80 a month.
head: What if checking in was as simple as turning on the TV?
hero: senior smiling at a friendly caregiver on the television

blocks.text: Not every season calls for hands-on care. Sometimes what an independent older adult needs is connection, a friendly face, a reminder, a set of eyes that notices when something changes. That's why we created HomeTogether™.
It plugs right into the television. No apps, no complicated setup, no tiny buttons. Just a warm check-in that appears on the screen they already know how to use.

items: 15-, 30-, or 60-minute visits
Friendly reminders for meals and medications
A gentle way to spot changes early
Peace of mind for family near and far

blocks.text: Starting at just $80 per month, and it pairs beautifully with in-home visits for “blended” care.

subj: You can't pour from an empty cup
pre: April is Stress Awareness Month. This one’s for you.
head: When was the last time someone took care of you?
hero: a tired family caregiver taking a quiet, restful moment

blocks.text: If you're the one keeping everything running, the appointments, the meals, the medications, the worry at 2 a.m., this note is for you, not your loved one.

Caregiver burnout is real, and it creeps in quietly: shorter patience, trouble sleeping, your own health sliding down the list. You are not failing. You are doing the work of several people, and you deserve relief.
That's what respite care is for. Whether it's a few hours a week or a weekend away, we'll step in so you can rest, recharge, and come back to your loved one as you, not just the caregiver.
Asking for help isn't giving up. It's how you keep going.

subj: Spring cleaning that could prevent a fall
pre: A few small changes around the house make a big difference.
head: This spring, clean for safety as much as for shine
hero: a bright, tidy, safe living room with clear walkways

blocks.text: Falls are the leading cause of injury for older adults, and most happen right at home, often over things we stop noticing after years in the same house. A little spring attention can prevent a lot of heartache.

items: Clear walkways of cords, clutter, and loose rugs
Add grab bars in the bathroom and good lighting in hallways
Keep everyday items within easy reach
Tidy garden paths and steps before mowing season

blocks.text: Our caregivers help with light housekeeping and these everyday safety habits, so home stays the safest place to be.

subj: For every mom who took care of us first
pre: This Mother’s Day, give her the gift of being cared for.
head: She spent a lifetime caring for everyone else
hero: a mother and her adult daughter embracing warmly

blocks.text: This Mother's Day, the flowers will be lovely, but the gift she may need most is the one that lasts past the weekend: knowing she's looked after, every day, by someone who treats her like their own.

For the mom or grandmother who raised you, a caregiver can mean warm meals, a clean home, rides to see friends, and company that brightens an ordinary Tuesday. Dignity, comfort, and a little joy, that's the gift.
Mom lights up when her caregiver arrives. It's the best decision our family ever made.
Happy Mother's Day from all of us at Caring Companions.

subj: Aging at home, on her own terms
pre: May is Older Americans Month, here’s to independence.
head: Independence doesn't have to mean doing it all alone
hero: an active senior gardening contentedly at home

blocks.text: This May, Older Americans Month celebrates a truth the years don't erase: older adults want to live on their own terms, in their own homes, surrounded by their own memories. Nearly everyone we serve says the same thing, “I just want to stay home.”

The right support makes that possible. Not by taking over, but by quietly filling the gaps, the heavy chores, the long drives, the tasks that have gotten harder, so the things that matter stay in your loved one's hands.
Care that grows with you: from a weekly check-in to around-the-clock support, your plan changes as needs change.

subj: The strong ones need a hand too
pre: This Father’s Day, make sure Dad is truly okay.
head: Dad spent his life being the strong one
hero: a father and adult son watching a ballgame together

blocks.text: Fathers are famously bad at asking for help. He'll tell you he's “fine,” wave off the cane, and insist he doesn't need anyone fussing over him. And maybe that independence is exactly why you worry.

A good caregiver knows how to help a proud man keep his dignity, a hand that looks like friendship, not fussing. Someone to share a ballgame with, keep the house in order, and quietly make sure he's safe.
This Father's Day, let him stay the strong, independent man he's always been, with just a little backup.

subj: Caring for someone whose memory is changing
pre: June is Alzheimer’s & Brain Awareness Month.
head: Memory care, with patience built in
hero: a caregiver gently holding hands with a senior

blocks.text: When a parent or spouse begins to forget, names, routines, sometimes you, it can be one of the loneliest experiences a family faces. This Alzheimer's & Brain Awareness Month is a reminder that you don't have to navigate it by yourself.

Our caregivers are trained in dementia and Alzheimer's care: gentle redirection, steady routines, and the patience to meet your loved one wherever they are today. We care for the whole person, not the diagnosis.

items: Safe, familiar daily routines
Calm support through confusion or agitation
Engaging activities that spark connection
Respite so family caregivers can breathe

blocks.text: Wherever you are on this road, we'll walk it with you.

subj: Independence is worth protecting, at every age
pre: Happy 4th from Caring Companions.
head: This week, we celebrate independence, including theirs
hero: a multi-generational family gathered on a summer porch

blocks.text: There's something fitting about July. As the fireworks celebrate our country's independence, it's a good moment to think about the independence of the people who raised us, and how to help them keep it.

For an older adult, independence isn't about doing everything alone. It's about staying in the home they love, keeping their routines, and making their own choices, with just enough support to make it safe.
That's the whole reason we do this work. Happy Independence Day, from our family to yours.

subj: Missouri summers are hard on older bodies
pre: Heat affects seniors faster, here’s how to help.
head: When the heat index climbs, older adults are most at risk
hero: a senior staying cool indoors with a glass of water

blocks.text: A Southwest Missouri July can be brutal, and aging bodies handle heat far less efficiently than younger ones. Many older adults don't feel thirsty until they're already dehydrated, and some keep the AC off out of habit or worry about the bill.

items: Encourage water throughout the day, not just at meals
Keep the home cool, and check that the AC actually works
Plan errands for the cooler morning hours
Watch for dizziness, confusion, or flushed, dry skin

blocks.text: Our caregivers keep an eye on all of it, hydration, comfort, and the warning signs, so a hot day stays just a hot day.

subj: Going on vacation? We'll stay with Mom.
pre: Respite care means everyone gets a break.
head: You deserve a real vacation, not a worried one
hero: a relaxed family happily heading off on vacation

blocks.text: Summer trips are wonderful, until you're the one who handles Mom's care and there's no one to cover. Too many families either cancel the trip or spend it on the phone, worrying.

Respite care is the answer. For a weekend or a couple of weeks, a trusted caregiver steps into your routine, same meals, same medications, same comforts, so your loved one is safe and content while you actually rest.
Respite isn't just for vacations. Even a few regular hours each week can protect a family caregiver's health and patience.

subj: “We didn't know how much we needed them”
pre: A Springfield family’s story.
head: Sometimes the right help changes everything
hero: a multi-generational family together and at ease at home

blocks.text: When families first call us, they're usually exhausted, a little guilty, and unsure if they're “ready.” We hear the same thing again and again a few months later: we wish we'd called sooner.
After Dad's fall, we were drowning. Caring Companions matched us with someone who felt like family within a week. He's safe, he's happy, and we're a family again instead of a care schedule.
Since 2017, more than 400 Southwest Missouri families have trusted us with the people they love most. We'd be honored to earn that trust with yours.

subj: Most falls are preventable. Really.
pre: September is Healthy Aging Month.
head: A fall can change everything, so let's prevent it
hero: a senior walking confidently with a caregiver’s support

blocks.text: September's Healthy Aging Month carries one message worth repeating: a single fall can undo years of independence in an instant. The good news is that most falls are preventable with a few steady habits and small changes at home.

items: Stay active, gentle movement keeps balance and strength
Review medications that can cause dizziness
Wear supportive, non-slip footwear (no loose slippers!)
Light the path from bed to bathroom for nighttime trips

blocks.text: Our caregivers help with all of it, steady support while walking, safer routines, and a watchful eye that catches hazards before they cause harm.

subj: The secret to great care? The right match.
pre: How we pair your loved one with the right caregiver.
head: Anyone can send a caregiver. We send the right one.
hero: a caregiver and senior who clearly enjoy each other

blocks.text: The difference between care that works and care that doesn't usually comes down to one thing: the person who walks through the door. A great match feels less like hiring help and more like welcoming a friend.
That's why we don't just fill a shift. Our five-step matching process considers personality, interests, skills, and lifestyle, so your loved one and their caregiver actually click.

items: A thoughtful assessment of needs and preferences
Matching on compatibility, not just availability
A flexible start, with room to adjust
Ongoing check-ins to keep it working

blocks.text: And if it's ever not the right fit? We'll make it right, no contracts, no hassle.

subj: As it cools down, a gentle reminder
pre: Cozy season is a good time to check in on the ones we love.
head: The season of slowing down, and looking a little closer
hero: a cozy autumn scene in a senior’s warm living room

blocks.text: There's a coziness to fall in the Ozarks, sweaters, soups, early evenings. It's also the season when families start gathering again, and when you may notice that the parent you visited last spring seems a little frailer, a little more forgetful, a little more alone.
If something tugs at you this autumn, trust it. A short conversation now can mean a warm, safe winter for someone you love, and real peace of mind for you.
Earlier nights mean more hours alone. A few caregiver visits a week can fill them with warmth and company.

subj: Get the house ready before the first freeze
pre: A little prep now prevents big problems later.
head: Before winter sets in, a few things worth checking
hero: a warm, well-prepared home interior heading into winter

blocks.text: Winter is hard on an older adult's home, and the tasks that keep it safe, clearing gutters, checking the furnace, salting the steps, get harder to do alone every year. A little preparation now prevents cold, costly, and dangerous surprises later.

items: Test the heating system and replace furnace filters
Check smoke and carbon-monoxide detectors
Stock up on essentials before icy weather hits
Have a plan for snow, ice, and slick steps

blocks.text: Our caregivers help keep daily life running through the hardest months, errands, meals, warm company, and a safe path to the door.

subj: This month, we see you
pre: November is National Family Caregivers Month.
head: To the family caregivers: thank you, and please rest
hero: a thanksgiving table surrounded by family

blocks.text: November is National Family Caregivers Month, and Thanksgiving sits right in the middle of it, which feels exactly right. So before the table is set, we want to say it plainly: the quiet, daily work you do for someone you love does not go unseen.

It's also the season when the work doubles, hosting, traveling, cooking, on top of everything you already carry. Please don't try to do all of it alone. Letting a caregiver cover a few hours isn't stepping back; it's how you make it to the holiday with something left to give.
Hosting this year? Respite care can cover the cooking and care so you can simply enjoy your family.
From our family to yours, thank you, and happy Thanksgiving.

subj: Honoring those who served, at home
pre: VA-funded home care for the veterans we owe so much.
head: They served our country. Now it's our turn to serve them.
hero: a veteran and a respectful caregiver in conversation

blocks.text: This Veterans Day, we're thinking of the men and women who gave so much, and of the families now caring for them. What many don't realize is that the care a veteran needs at home may be available at little or no cost through VA programs.
Caring Companions works with VA Community Care and veteran in-home care programs to bring trained, compassionate caregivers to those who served, with help navigating the benefits along the way.

items: VA Community Care and veteran in-home care programs
Help understanding what your loved one qualifies for
Caregivers who honor their service and their dignity

blocks.text: To every veteran in our community: thank you. It would be our privilege to care for you.

subj: Home for the holidays? Keep your eyes open.
pre: Holiday visits often reveal what phone calls hide.
head: The holidays bring us home, and show us what's really going on
hero: a family gathered warmly around a Christmas living room

blocks.text: For many families, the holidays are the one time everyone's under the same roof again. And every year, it's during these visits that adult children realize Mom or Dad needs more help than the weekly phone calls let on.
As you gather this season, notice gently:

items: Is the home as clean and well-stocked as it used to be?
Are they steady on their feet, and keeping up with medications?
Has there been weight loss, or unexplained bruises?
Do they seem more isolated or forgetful than last year?

blocks.text: If you see something that worries you, you don't have to solve it overnight. Start with a conversation, with them, and with us. We'll help you figure out the next gentle step.
We keep caregivers available through the holidays, so support can start whenever your family needs it.

sign: Merry Christmas from our family to yours,

subj: The gift that lasts long after the holidays
pre: This year, give peace of mind.
head: The best gift this year isn't under the tree
hero: a warm winter window glowing with holiday lights

blocks.text: As another year winds down, the gifts that matter most aren't wrapped, they're the comfort of knowing someone you love is safe, cared for, and not alone. That's a gift you can give all year long.

Whether it's a few companionship hours a week, hands-on daily care, or a HomeTogether™ check-in through the TV, we'll build a plan that fits your family, with no contracts and the freedom to change it anytime.
Thank you for letting us be part of so many Southwest Missouri families this year. Here's to a healthy, warm, and connected new year.

sign: Warm wishes for the new year,

subj: How are we doing? We'd love to hear from you
pre: A quick winter hello, and a small favor.
head: A new year, and a little check-in

blocks.text: Happy New Year! As we settle into another year together, we wanted to pause and simply ask: how are we doing? You are the reason we do what we do, and your comfort and happiness matter to us more than anything.
If there's something that would make your days easier, a different routine, a little more help, or a change you'd like, please tell us. There are no wrong answers, and nothing is ever too small.
And don't worry about doing this alone, your caregiver is always glad to help you share your thoughts.

subj: Your quarterly check-in, how are we doing?
pre: Two minutes to tell us how care is going.
head: Checking in as the new year begins

blocks.text: As we begin another year caring for your loved one, we'd be grateful for your honest feedback. You notice things we may not, and your perspective helps us keep their care exactly where it should be.
Would you take two minutes to let us know how we're doing, from the quality of care to how well we keep you informed and involved?
Think your loved one could use a little more support this winter? We can adjust hours or add services at any time, just reply or give us a call.

subj: A spring hello, and how are we doing?
pre: Checking in as the days grow longer.
head: Spring is here, let's check in

blocks.text: The days are getting longer and brighter, and we wanted to check in with you. More than anything, we want to be sure you're happy, comfortable, and well cared for.
Is there anything we could be doing better, or anything you'd like more of? Your voice shapes your care, and we'd truly love to hear it.
Your caregiver can help you fill this out any time, just ask.

subj: Spring check-in: how is care going?
pre: A quick survey on your loved one's care.
head: A spring check-in on your loved one's care

blocks.text: As spring arrives, we'd love a quick read on how things are going for your loved one. Your feedback helps us catch the little things early and keep their care feeling just right.
Warmer weather means more chances to get out. If your loved one would enjoy help with errands, appointments, or outings, we're happy to add it.

subj: A summer hello from your Caring Companions family
pre: Checking in, and keeping you cool.
head: How are we doing this summer?

blocks.text: We hope you're staying cool and comfortable this summer. As always, we want to make sure your care feels just right, so we wanted to check in and ask how we're doing.
If anything would make your days easier or more enjoyable, please let us know. We're always here to adjust.

subj: Summer check-in: how is care going?
pre: Two minutes on your loved one's care.
head: Checking in on care this summer

blocks.text: Summer's heat can be hard on older adults, so our caregivers keep a close eye on hydration, comfort, and cool surroundings. We'd also love your feedback on how care is going overall.
Traveling this summer? We can add hours, overnight care, or respite so your loved one is well looked after while you're away.

subj: A year together, thank you
pre: Looking back, and checking in.
head: As the year winds down, a heartfelt check-in

blocks.text: It's been our privilege to be part of your days this year. As things slow down for the season, we wanted to pause and ask how you're feeling about your care, and whether there's anything we can do to serve you even better.
Thank you for welcoming us into your home and your life. It means more to us than you know.

subj: Annual care review, let's look ahead together
pre: Reflect on this year and plan for the next.
head: Time for your loved one's annual care review

blocks.text: As the year comes to a close, it's a good moment to step back and review how your loved one's care is working, what's going well, what's changed, and what they may need in the year ahead.
We'd love your feedback below, and we're always glad to schedule a call to revisit the care plan together.
Needs often shift over a year. If you've noticed changes, we can update the plan, more hours, added services, or specialized support.

subj: Happy New Year from all of us
pre: Here's to a year of comfort and good company.
head: Wishing you a warm and happy New Year

blocks.text: From your whole Caring Companions family, happy New Year! We feel lucky to start another year alongside you, and we're looking forward to all the ordinary, wonderful days ahead.
Here's to a year filled with comfort, good company, and feeling right at home, because that's exactly what you deserve.

subj: Happy New Year, thank you for trusting us
pre: Grateful to care for your loved one another year.
head: Wishing your family a happy and healthy New Year

blocks.text: As a new year begins, we want to say thank you. Trusting us with someone you love is no small thing, and we don't take it for granted.
Here's to a healthy, comfortable year ahead for your loved one, and please remember, as their needs change, we can grow their care right along with them.
A new year is a natural time to revisit the care plan. If you'd like to talk through any adjustments, we're just a call away.

subj: You're family to us
pre: A little Valentine's note, just for you.
head: This Valentine's Day, we're thinking of you

blocks.text: Valentine's Day is about the people who fill our lives with warmth, and you're one of those people for us. It's a joy to spend our days with you.
Thank you for your stories, your smiles, and for letting us be part of your life. Happy Valentine's Day.

cta.label: We're Always Just a Call Away

subj: Thinking of your loved one this Valentine's Day
pre: A note of appreciation, and a gentle thought.
head: Sending a little extra warmth this Valentine's

blocks.text: This Valentine's Day, we're grateful for the chance to care for someone you love. We hope they feel the warmth and companionship of the season.
If your loved one ever feels lonely between visits, especially in these quieter winter months, a few extra companionship hours or a HomeTogether™ check-in can brighten their days. Just say the word.

subj: Happy 4th of July!
pre: Warm wishes for a safe, happy holiday.
head: Wishing you a happy Independence Day

blocks.text: Happy Fourth of July! We hope your day is filled with good food, familiar faces, and a little celebration. However you spend it, know we're thinking of you.
And as the summer heats up, please remember to stay cool and hydrated, your caregiver is always here to help.

subj: Happy 4th, and a note on holiday support
pre: Holiday wishes and support options.
head: Wishing your family a happy Independence Day

blocks.text: Happy Fourth of July from all of us! We hope your family enjoys a wonderful holiday together.
Traveling or hosting this summer? We can add hours, overnight, or respite care so your loved one stays safe and comfortable, and you can relax. Just let us know.
Our caregivers are also keeping a close eye on summer heat and hydration for your loved one.

subj: We're thankful for you
pre: A Thanksgiving note from your care team.
head: This Thanksgiving, we're grateful for you

blocks.text: When we count our blessings this Thanksgiving, you're near the top of the list. It's a true gift to spend our days with you, and we're thankful for every moment.
We hope your holiday is warm, comfortable, and full of the people and things you love.

cta.label: We're Always Just a Call Away

subj: Grateful to care for your loved one this Thanksgiving
pre: Thank you, and a little holiday help if you need it.
head: This Thanksgiving, thank you for trusting us

blocks.text: This season of gratitude, we want you to know how thankful we are for the trust you place in us to care for someone so important to your family.
Hosting, traveling, or simply stretched thin this Thanksgiving? Extra caregiver hours or respite can lighten the load so you can be present with your family. We'd be glad to help.

subj: Merry Christmas and happy holidays
pre: Warm wishes for a cozy, joyful season.
head: Wishing you a warm and joyful holiday season

blocks.text: From all of us at Caring Companions, we're wishing you the merriest, coziest holiday season. May your days be filled with warmth, good memories, and the people who love you.
And if the season ever feels quiet, remember, you're never alone. We're always just a phone call away.

subj: Warm holiday wishes, and a little extra help if you need it
pre: Season's greetings and holiday support options.
head: Happy holidays from your loved one's care team

blocks.text: Wishing your whole family a warm and joyful holiday season. It's been our honor to care for your loved one this year.

The holidays can be a lot, hosting, traveling, extra visitors. If you'd like additional hours, overnight care, or respite so everyone can enjoy the season, we keep caregivers available right through the holidays.
Whatever you need, we're here. Merry Christmas and happy holidays.

subj: A friendly face on your TV between visits
pre: Meet HomeTogether™, simple and warm.
head: What if we could check in, even between visits?

blocks.text: You know how nice it is to have your caregiver around. HomeTogether™ is a way to see a friendly face even on the days we're not there in person, right on your television.

There's nothing to set up and no tricky buttons. At your scheduled time, a warm, familiar face simply appears on your TV to say hello, share a little company, and remind you about meals or medications.
It works right alongside your regular visits, a little extra connection and peace of mind, starting at $80 a month.

subj: An easy way to add support between visits
pre: How HomeTogether™ complements your loved one's care.
head: Meet HomeTogether™, care that fills the gaps

blocks.text: You already trust us with your loved one's in-home care. HomeTogether™ is a simple add-on that extends that support to the hours in between, a friendly, scheduled check-in delivered right through their television.
It's an easy way to reduce isolation, keep routines on track, and catch small changes sooner, without anything for your loved one to learn or install.

items: 15-, 30-, or 60-minute virtual visits
Friendly reminders for meals and medications
Changes spotted earlier, between in-person visits
More peace of mind for you, near or far

blocks.text: Combine it with current care for true “blended” support, starting at just $80 a month.

subj: Want a little extra company this season?
pre: More help is here whenever you'd like it.
head: The holidays are merrier with company

blocks.text: The holidays can be busy and bright, or sometimes just a bit quiet. Whichever they are for you this year, we'd love to make them warmer.
If you'd enjoy some extra company, a hand with holiday cooking, help getting to gatherings, or just a few more visits, all you have to do is ask. We're here for you.

subj: Extra support for your loved one this holiday season
pre: Add hours, overnight, or respite, we keep caregivers available.
head: A little extra help can make the holidays easier

blocks.text: The holidays often bring more to juggle, travel, hosting, visitors, and a busier schedule for everyone. They can also be a lonelier, more disorienting time for older adults.
We're here to help however you need. A few options families love this time of year:

items: Extra daytime hours for company and holiday prep
Overnight or 24-hour care while you travel or host
Respite care so the whole family can be present
Transportation to gatherings and appointments

blocks.text: Caregivers book up quickly around the holidays, the earlier you let us know, the better we can plan.

subj: A few more ways we're here for you
pre: Did you know we can help with all of this?
head: More ways we can help, whenever you need

blocks.text: You may know us for the help you receive today, but there's a lot more we can do whenever you'd like. We're always ready to do more, never less.

items: Rides to appointments, errands, and the store
Help with meals, light housekeeping, and laundry
A nurse visit for medication management
Bathing, dressing, and personal care support

blocks.text: If any of these would make life easier, just mention it to your caregiver or give us a call.

subj: Did you know we offer all of this?
pre: Care that can grow with your loved one's needs.
head: More ways we can support your loved one

blocks.text: As your loved one's needs change, their care can change right along with them, no new agency, no starting over. Here are some of the ways we can do more:

items: Private-duty nursing and medication management
Personal care, bathing, dressing, mobility
Respite care to support family caregivers
Transition care after a hospital stay or illness
Specialized care for dementia, Parkinson's, and more
VA, Medicaid, and long-term care insurance options

blocks.text: Noticing your loved one needs a little more lately? Let's revisit the plan, we'll make it seamless.

subj: How are we doing as your team?
pre: Your voice shapes how we support you.
head: Let's start the year by hearing from you

blocks.text: Happy New Year! Before the year gets busy, we want to hear from you. You're the heart of everything we do at Caring Companions, and we're always working to be a better team to work with.

How are we doing? Are you getting the support, communication, and schedule that work for you? Tell us what's going well and what we could do better, your honest feedback is always welcome and always confidential.
And thank you for the care you give every single day. It does not go unnoticed.

subj: Spring check-in: how can we support you better?
pre: A quick pulse-check with your team.
head: Checking in this spring

blocks.text: As the days warm up, we wanted to pause and check in with you. Your wellbeing matters to us just as much as the care you provide.
Is there anything that would make your work easier, scheduling, training, equipment, or simply more support from the office? Let us know below.
We're grateful for you, this season and every season.

subj: Summer check-in, and a big thank you
pre: How are we doing as your employer?
head: A midyear hello from the office

blocks.text: Summer can be a demanding season, heat, travel, busier client needs. We want to make sure you feel supported through all of it.
How are we doing? Share your honest feedback so we can keep improving as a team and as an employer.
Working in the heat? Please stay hydrated and take your breaks, your health comes first, always.

subj: Looking back on the year, how did we do?
pre: Your feedback shapes next year.
head: As the year winds down, we want to hear from you

blocks.text: What a year it's been. Before it wraps up, we'd love your reflections on how this year went, the good, the hard, and anything you'd change.
Your feedback genuinely shapes the decisions we make about scheduling, training, and support in the year ahead.
Thank you for everything you've given our clients and our team this year.

hero: new year wishes for the team
subj: Happy New Year to our incredible team
pre: Here's to you, and the year ahead.
head: Wishing you a happy and healthy New Year

blocks.text: As a new year begins, we want to say thank you. The compassion you bring into our clients' homes changes lives, and you are what makes Caring Companions what it is.
Here's to a year of good health, good shifts, and feeling appreciated for the essential work you do.

subj: You bring so much heart to this work
pre: A little Valentine's thank-you for our caregivers.
head: This Valentine's Day, we're celebrating you

blocks.text: Valentine's Day is about heart, and few people give more of theirs than our caregivers. The warmth, patience, and kindness you show our clients is the truest kind of love there is.
Thank you for the heart you bring to work every day. We're so grateful for you.

subj: Happy 4th of July, and thank you
pre: A holiday thank-you to those caring for others.
head: Wishing you a happy Independence Day

blocks.text: Happy Fourth of July! Whether you're off enjoying the holiday or working to keep our clients safe and cared for, we're thinking of you and grateful for you.
Covering a holiday shift? Thank you, truly. Reach out to the office anytime if you need anything while you're on.

subj: This Thanksgiving, we're thankful for you
pre: Our caregivers are at the top of our gratitude list.
head: Grateful for you this Thanksgiving

blocks.text: When we count our blessings this Thanksgiving, our caregivers are right at the top of the list. The comfort and dignity you bring to our clients is a gift to entire families.
Thank you for all you do, especially in a season when your care means even more. Happy Thanksgiving.

subj: Happy holidays to our caregiving family
pre: Warm wishes, and thanks for the shifts that matter most.
head: Wishing you a warm and joyful holiday season

blocks.text: From all of us at Caring Companions, happy holidays! We hope your season is filled with rest, warmth, and the people you love.
To everyone covering holiday shifts so our clients aren't alone, thank you. It's one of the most meaningful things anyone can do, and we don't take it for granted.

subj: It's Caregiver Appreciation Week, this one's for you
pre: Celebrating the people at the heart of our work.
head: A whole week isn't enough to thank you

blocks.text: This Caregiver Appreciation Week, we want to say what we hope you feel all year: you are extraordinary. The work you do is hard, intimate, and deeply human, and you do it with grace.
Every safe bath, every shared meal, every hand held, it adds up to something that matters enormously. Thank you for choosing this work, and for choosing to do it here.
Our caregivers are the reason families trust us. You make the difference, every single day.

subj: Know a caregiver who goes above and beyond?
pre: Nominations for Caregiver of the Year are open.
head: Help us honor our Caregiver of the Year

blocks.text: Every year, we recognize a caregiver who truly goes above and beyond, in care quality, family engagement, in-home excellence, and the way they show up for our community.
Who comes to mind? Nominate a coworker (or yourself!) who embodies the very best of Caring Companions. We can't wait to celebrate them.
Nominations are open through the end of the month, and every one is read by our leadership team.

subj: A reminder: your wellbeing matters too
pre: Caring for yourself helps you care for others.
head: You can't pour from an empty cup

blocks.text: You spend your days taking care of everyone else. This is a gentle reminder to take care of you, too, because you matter, and because rested, supported caregivers give the very best care.

items: Take your breaks, and actually rest during them
Stay hydrated and eat real meals on shift
Set boundaries; it's okay to say you need a day
Lean on the office, we're here for you, not just the schedule

blocks.text: Feeling stretched thin or burned out? Please talk to your care coordinator. We'd always rather support you than lose you.

subj: Know someone who'd make a great caregiver?
pre: Refer a friend, and earn a referral bonus.
head: The best caregivers know other great caregivers

blocks.text: You know better than anyone what it takes to do this work well, heart, patience, and reliability. If you know someone like that, we'd love to meet them.
Refer a friend to join our team, and you'll earn a referral bonus once they're hired and start caring for clients. It's our way of saying thanks for helping us grow the right way.
Great teams are built on great referrals. Thank you for helping us find people as wonderful as you.

subj: A trusted in-home partner for your hospice families
pre: Non-medical support that complements hospice care.
head: Helping your hospice patients stay comfortable at home

blocks.text: At Caring Companions, we partner with hospice teams across Southwest Missouri to surround patients and families with support between clinical visits. Your nurses and aides provide expert care; our caregivers provide the hours in between, personal care, companionship, and a compassionate, watchful presence.

items: Assistance with bathing, dressing, and daily activities
Companionship plus overnight and 24-hour care
Respite so exhausted family caregivers can rest
Vetted, trained, and insured caregivers

blocks.text: We'd welcome the chance to introduce ourselves and learn how we can make your patients' final months more comfortable.

subj: How we support hospice families, around the clock
pre: Respite, ADLs, and presence when families need it most.
head: A seamless extension of your hospice team

blocks.text: When a family chooses hospice, the emotional and physical demands on them are immense. Our caregivers step in to carry the everyday load, so families can focus on being present with the people they love.
We coordinate closely with your team, communicate changes promptly, and adapt as needs evolve, day or night.
Need coverage tonight? We keep caregivers available 24/7, including overnights and weekends.

cta.label: Let's Set Up a Lunch & Learn

subj: Where home health ends, we keep the care going
pre: Non-medical support after skilled services wrap up.
head: A safe landing when skilled care ends

blocks.text: Your team gets patients back on their feet, but when home health benefits end, many patients still aren't ready to manage alone. That gap is where readmissions happen. Caring Companions fills it with dependable non-medical support.

items: Personal care, mobility, and medication reminders
Meal prep, light housekeeping, and errands
Transportation to follow-up appointments
Flexible hours, from a few a week to 24/7

blocks.text: Let's talk about how a warm handoff to our team can protect your patients' recovery.

subj: Filling the gaps between skilled visits
pre: The day-to-day support that keeps recovery on track.
head: The hours your patients are on their own

blocks.text: Skilled visits are essential, and brief. It's the other 23 hours a day that often determine whether a patient stays safe and on the mend. Our caregivers provide consistent, hands-on help during exactly those hours.
We can start quickly, coordinate with your plan of care, and report changes back to the family and your team.
No contracts and fully customizable hours, so support scales up or down as your patient progresses.

subj: Help your residents stay, even as their needs grow
pre: Private-duty support that complements your community.
head: A care partner that helps residents age in place

blocks.text: When a resident's needs outpace what your community provides, the conversation often turns to moving them out. Caring Companions offers another path: private-duty caregivers who provide one-on-one support right in their apartment, helping residents stay where they're happy.

items: One-on-one personal care and companionship
Extra support after a fall, illness, or hospital stay
Overnight and 24-hour coverage when needed
A trusted, insured partner your families can rely on

blocks.text: We'd love to be a resource your team can call on. Could we schedule a brief introduction?

subj: A reliable care partner for your community
pre: On-call support that keeps residents safe and families happy.
head: Support your residents, without adding to your staff

blocks.text: Your team does so much already. When a resident needs more one-on-one attention than your staffing allows, we're here to fill in, reliably, professionally, and without disrupting your community's rhythm.
From a few hours of companionship to around-the-clock care, we coordinate directly with families and keep your team informed.
We're happy to host a lunch & learn for your staff so they know exactly when and how to bring us in.

cta.label: Let's Set Up a Lunch & Learn

subj: Safe discharges home start with the right support
pre: Transition care that helps prevent readmissions.
head: Get your patients home, and keep them there

blocks.text: A successful discharge doesn't end at the door. Many patients return to the hospital simply because they couldn't manage at home alone. Caring Companions provides the transition support that makes 'home' the safe choice.

items: Recovery and rehabilitation support at home
Medication reminders and appointment transportation
Help with bathing, mobility, and daily tasks
Same-week starts to keep discharges on schedule

blocks.text: We'd value the chance to be part of your discharge resources. May we schedule a quick introduction?

subj: Reducing readmissions with transition care
pre: The first 30 days home, fully supported.
head: The riskiest weeks are the first ones home

blocks.text: The 30 days after discharge are when readmissions are most likely, and most preventable. Our caregivers provide the consistent presence that catches problems early: missed medications, unsteady mobility, poor nutrition, or a home that isn't quite ready.
We can begin the day of discharge, coordinate with your plan, and keep families and your team in the loop.
Flexible, no-contract hours mean support can taper as your patient regains independence.

hero: carrying out the care plan at home
subj: Extra eyes and hands for your patients at home
pre: In-home support that helps your care plan succeed.
head: Your care plan, carried out at home

blocks.text: You can write the best care plan in the world, but it only works if it's followed at home. Caring Companions helps your older patients stick to it: taking medications on time, eating well, staying mobile, and getting to follow-ups.

items: Medication and appointment reminders
Help with daily activities, mobility, and nutrition
A consistent caregiver who notices changes early
Communication back to family and your office

blocks.text: We'd be glad to introduce our services so your team knows we're here when a patient needs more support at home.

subj: When a patient needs more than a prescription
pre: A trusted referral for non-medical home support.
head: For the patients you worry about going home

blocks.text: Every clinician has those patients, the ones who are medically stable but clearly struggling to manage at home. For them, a few hours of caregiver support a week can be as impactful as any prescription.
We help with the daily realities that affect health outcomes: nutrition, medication adherence, fall prevention, and isolation, and we keep you informed of meaningful changes.
Private pay, long-term care insurance, VA, and Medicaid options help make care accessible for your patients.

subj: A dependable discharge partner in Southwest Missouri
pre: Same-week, non-medical support for safer discharges.
head: A discharge resource you can count on

blocks.text: When you're arranging a safe discharge, you need partners who answer the phone and show up. Caring Companions has served Southwest Missouri families since 2017, with the responsiveness and capacity to support your discharges, often the same week.

items: Transition care to reduce avoidable readmissions
24/7 availability, including nights and weekends
Help navigating private pay, VA, LTC, and Medicaid
Vetted, trained, insured caregivers you can trust

blocks.text: Let's connect so we're ready the next time you need a reliable hand at discharge.

hero: care your clients can count on
subj: Care your clients can count on
pre: A trusted in-home care partner for your clients and their families.
head: When your clients need care, send them to people you trust

blocks.text: Your clients turn to you at pivotal moments, and often, what they need next is dependable care at home. Caring Companions is a partner you can refer with confidence: established, compassionate, and experienced in the funding questions your clients face.

items: Guidance on private pay, LTC insurance, VA, and Medicaid
Help families understand what care actually costs
Flexible, no-contract plans that fit any budget
A responsive team that makes you look good for referring us

blocks.text: We'd welcome a conversation about how we can support your clients together.

subj: Introducing Caring Companions In-Home Senior Care
pre: A trusted home-care partner in Springfield & SW Missouri.
head: Compassionate in-home care, since 2017

blocks.text: Caring Companions has cared for more than 400 families across Springfield and Southwest Missouri since 2017. We provide a full continuum of non-medical home care, from a few hours of companionship to 24/7 support, and we'd love to be a resource for the people you serve.

items: Companionship, personal care, and homemaking
Transition care, overnight, and live-in options
Specialized support for dementia and complex conditions
Private pay, LTC insurance, VA, and Medicaid options

blocks.text: Whenever someone you work with needs care at home, we'd be honored to help, and to make you glad you referred us.

subj: Thank you for trusting us with your referral
pre: We're honored you thought of us.
head: Your referral means the world to us

blocks.text: Thank you for referring your client to Caring Companions. We don't take that trust lightly, a referral puts your reputation on the line, and we're committed to honoring it with exceptional care.
We'll keep you informed as appropriate, and we're always just a phone call away if you'd like an update or have another family who needs us.
Know someone else who could use our support? We're here, and we're grateful for every introduction.

subj: We have caregiver availability this week
pre: Capacity now for new clients you refer.
head: Have a family who needs care soon? We have openings

blocks.text: A quick note to let you know we currently have caregiver availability and can take on new clients, often within the same week, including evenings, overnights, and weekends.
If you're working with a family who needs support at home, we're ready to help right away. Just call or send them our way.
Even urgent, same-week starts are usually possible, please don't hesitate to reach out.

subj: Checking in from your friends at Caring Companions
pre: A quick hello and thank-you to our referral partners.
head: Just checking in, how can we support you?

blocks.text: It's been a little while, so we wanted to reach out and say thank you for being a valued part of our referral community. Our goal is to make every family you send us glad you did.
Is there anything we can do to serve you and your clients better, more information, a lunch & learn, or a refresher on what we offer? We're all ears.
We always have capacity updates and new resources to share, reply anytime and we'll be in touch.

subj: More than companionship: our full continuum of care
pre: Care that scales from a few hours to 24/7, alongside hospice.
head: Whatever your hospice patients need, we can flex to meet it

blocks.text: Families on hospice rarely need just one kind of help. Caring Companions offers a full continuum, from a few hours of companionship to overnight and around-the-clock care, and we adjust as a patient's needs change, often day by day.

items: Companionship and supervision
Hands-on personal care and daily activities
Overnight and 24-hour coverage
Specialized support for complex, end-of-life needs

blocks.text: Whenever a hospice family needs more support at home, referring them takes just a minute.

subj: How families pay for in-home support during hospice
pre: Funding options that keep care within reach.
head: Cost shouldn't keep a hospice family from extra help

blocks.text: The hospice benefit covers so much, but not the around-the-clock personal care many families wish they had. When families ask how they can afford more help at home, we can walk them through every option.

items: Private pay, with flexible, no-contract hours
Long-term care insurance
VA Community Care for eligible veterans
Missouri Medicaid home- and community-based programs

blocks.text: We're glad to help your families understand what's possible, just send them our way.

subj: A simple way to add check-ins between nurse visits
pre: HomeTogether™, virtual support through the TV, $80/mo.
head: Eyes on your hospice patients, between visits

blocks.text: Between clinical visits, a lot can change. HomeTogether™ adds friendly virtual check-ins right through the patient's television, a warm face, a reminder, and a chance to spot changes early, with no app or setup.
Just $80 a month, and it pairs with in-home hours for blended support, an affordable option for budget-conscious families.

subj: A continuum of care that scales with recovery
pre: Non-medical care that grows or tapers as patients improve.
head: From intensive support to a light touch, we flex with your patients

blocks.text: Recovery isn't linear, and support shouldn't be rigid. Our continuum lets patients start with more hands-on help right after your skilled care, then taper to companionship and check-ins as they regain independence.

items: Transition and recovery support
Personal care and mobility assistance
Homemaking, meals, and transportation
Companionship and wellness check-ins

blocks.text: Referring a patient who needs ongoing support is quick and secure.

subj: What happens when home health benefits run out?
pre: Funding options for continued support at home.
head: When skilled benefits end, families still have options

blocks.text: When Medicare home health ends, families often don't realize ongoing non-medical care is within reach. We help them understand how to pay for it so recovery doesn't stall.

items: Private pay with flexible hours
Long-term care insurance
VA Community Care
Missouri Medicaid home- and community-based programs

blocks.text: Point them our way and we'll handle the rest.

subj: Keep an eye on patients after home health ends
pre: HomeTogether™, virtual monitoring through the TV, $80/mo.
head: Affordable check-ins to protect your patients' progress

blocks.text: After discharge from home health, HomeTogether™ keeps a friendly, watchful presence in the picture, virtual visits through the TV with medication reminders and early change detection, for $80 a month.
It's an easy, low-cost step-down that helps prevent the slide back into the hospital.

subj: One-on-one care that grows with your residents
pre: Private-duty support across a full continuum.
head: Help residents stay, even as their needs increase

blocks.text: As residents age in place, their needs evolve. Our continuum, from companionship to 24-hour care, lets them get exactly the one-on-one help they need, right in their apartment, without leaving the community they love.

items: Companionship and supervision
Personal care and mobility help
Overnight and 24-hour coverage
Specialized dementia support

blocks.text: When a resident needs more than your team can provide, referring them is simple and secure.

subj: Helping your residents' families fund extra care
pre: Payment options that fit any budget.
head: Extra one-on-one care, within reach for your families

blocks.text: When families consider private-duty support on top of their community fees, cost is the first question. We help them navigate every option so the answer is yes.

items: Private pay, no contracts
Long-term care insurance
VA Community Care
Missouri Medicaid home- and community-based programs

blocks.text: We're happy to be your go-to resource for these conversations.

subj: A low-cost way to supplement community support
pre: HomeTogether™, virtual check-ins, $80/mo.
head: Extra connection for residents, without extra staff

blocks.text: HomeTogether™ gives residents friendly virtual check-ins through their TV, reminders, company, and early awareness of changes, complementing your community's care for just $80 a month.
A simple add-on for residents who need a little more connection between your staff's rounds.

subj: A continuum of care for safer discharges home
pre: Support that scales from recovery to independence.
head: Match the right level of care to each discharge

blocks.text: Some patients go home needing intensive help; others just need a safety net. Our continuum lets you discharge with confidence, knowing support can be as much or as little as each patient requires, and can change as they recover.

items: Transition and rehabilitation support
Personal care and mobility assistance
Medication reminders and transportation
Wellness check-ins as independence returns

blocks.text: Referring a discharge is quick, secure, and can be same-week.

subj: When SNF days run out, what comes next?
pre: Funding options for support after rehab.
head: Help patients keep recovering after covered days end

blocks.text: When Medicare's covered rehab days end, families often scramble. We help them understand how to fund continued support at home so a safe discharge stays safe.

items: Private pay with flexible hours
Long-term care insurance
VA Community Care
Missouri Medicaid home- and community-based programs

blocks.text: Send them our way and we'll guide them through it.

subj: Cut readmission risk with virtual check-ins
pre: HomeTogether™, post-discharge monitoring, $80/mo.
head: A low-cost safety net for the first weeks home

blocks.text: HomeTogether™ adds virtual check-ins through the patient's TV during the riskiest post-discharge weeks, medication reminders, a friendly face, and early detection of trouble, for $80 a month.
An affordable layer of oversight that helps keep patients out of the hospital.

subj: In-home support matched to your patients' acuity
pre: A continuum from companionship to complex care.
head: The right level of home support for every patient

blocks.text: Your patients' needs range widely, and so does our care. From a few companionship hours to 24/7 and specialized dementia support, we match the level of help to each patient and adjust as their condition changes.

items: Companionship and wellness oversight
Personal care and mobility help
Medication reminders and transportation
Specialized and complex-condition care

blocks.text: Referring a patient who needs more support at home takes just a minute, securely.

subj: Helping your patients afford care at home
pre: Funding options to discuss with families.
head: Cost is the first question families ask, we have answers

blocks.text: When you recommend home support, families want to know how they'll pay for it. We help them understand every avenue so your recommendation actually happens.

items: Private pay, no contracts
Long-term care insurance
VA Community Care
Missouri Medicaid programs and the Medicare GUIDE program

blocks.text: Refer them to us and we'll take the funding conversation from there.

subj: Remote oversight for your at-risk patients
pre: HomeTogether™, virtual visits & reminders, $80/mo.
head: A simple way to extend oversight into the home

blocks.text: HomeTogether™ brings friendly virtual visits to your patients through their TV, medication reminders, routine check-ins, and early awareness of changes you'd want to know about, for $80 a month.
An affordable option for patients who need more monitoring than family can provide.

subj: A full continuum for even your most complex discharges
pre: Support that scales to any level of need.
head: From a safety net to 24/7, we can match the discharge

blocks.text: Complex discharges need flexible support. Our continuum spans companionship to around-the-clock and specialized care, so you can send patients home knowing the right level of help is in place, and can adapt.

items: Transition and recovery support
Personal care and 24-hour coverage
Medication reminders and transportation
Specialized dementia and complex-condition care

blocks.text: Referrals are secure and often same-week.

subj: Helping families fund care at discharge
pre: Payor navigation that keeps discharges on track.
head: Don't let funding questions delay a safe discharge

blocks.text: At discharge, funding confusion can stall everything. We help families quickly understand their options so they can say yes to the support they need.

items: Private pay with flexible hours
Long-term care insurance
VA Community Care
Missouri Medicaid home- and community-based programs

blocks.text: Refer the family and we'll handle the details.

subj: Post-discharge monitoring that reduces readmissions
pre: HomeTogether™, virtual check-ins, $80/mo.
head: An affordable safety net for discharged patients

blocks.text: HomeTogether™ adds virtual check-ins through the patient's TV after discharge, reminders, a friendly presence, and early detection, for $80 a month, helping protect your readmission numbers.
Easy to set up, with nothing for the patient to learn.

subj: Care that protects your clients' plans over time
pre: A continuum that adapts as needs and budgets change.
head: Flexible care that fits the plans you build

blocks.text: The care plans you help families fund need to flex over time. Our continuum, from a few hours to 24/7, lets clients use exactly what they need, preserving resources while keeping them safe at home.

items: Companionship through complex care
No contracts, fully customizable hours
Care that scales up or down with the plan
A partner who keeps families informed

blocks.text: Refer a client securely whenever care is the next step.

subj: Navigating LTC insurance, VA, and Medicaid with you
pre: A care partner fluent in the funding you handle.
head: We speak your clients' funding language

blocks.text: You handle the legal and financial strategy; we make the care side just as smooth. We're experienced with the payors your clients rely on and help them put benefits to work.

items: Long-term care insurance billing and coordination
VA Community Care for veterans
Missouri Medicaid spend-down and HCBS
Private pay with transparent, no-contract terms

blocks.text: Together, we can make the transition to care seamless for your clients.

subj: An affordable care option for budget-conscious clients
pre: HomeTogether™, virtual support, $80/mo.
head: When clients want help without a big spend

blocks.text: Not every client is ready for hours of in-home care. HomeTogether™ offers a meaningful, low-cost alternative, friendly virtual check-ins through the TV with reminders and oversight, for $80 a month.
A budget-friendly first step that can grow into more care as needs increase.

subj: The easiest, most secure way to refer a patient
pre: Send a referral in two minutes, safely and confidentially.
head: Referring a patient takes just two minutes

blocks.text: We've made referring to Caring Companions simple and secure. Our online referral form is confidential and HIPAA-conscious, just share the basics, and our care coordinators take it from there, keeping you informed along the way.

items: Quick, secure online form, no phone tag
We follow up with the family right away
You stay in the loop on next steps
Or call us anytime at (417) 234-8494

blocks.text: Have a patient or client who needs care at home? We're ready to help.
